title: Rebuild of Evangelion 111 US release March 9th 2010
display_title: Rebuild of Evangelion 111 US release March 9th 2010
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild of Evangelion 1.11 U.S release: March 9th 2010             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:40 pm DVD: http://www.rightstuf.com/cgi-bin/catalogmgr/qXLRhc-GotoYkRchg3/browse/item/86136/4/0/0 March 9th 2010, w00t! BLU-RAY! http://www.rightstuf.com/cgi-bin/catalogmgr/qXLRhc-GotoYkRchg3/browse/item/86135/4/0/0

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:47 pm Hopefully I will only have to wait a month or so to see an Australian release then.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:51 pm That's great news, yet they don't seem to have it on Amazon yet. If this proves to be true, I'll be very happy.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:52 pm News posted: http://www.evageeks.org/2009/12/1-11-released-on-march-9th-2010/

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sun Dec 20, 2009 11:04 pm *makes room on shelf for another Eva related dvd*

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 20, 2009 11:34 pm Looks conveniently timed with my birthday. I'm going to feel weird owning 2-3 copies of this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 21, 2009 12:46 am Versions on my PC and 1.01 DVD aside, I think I might just wait for a Gainax/Kara tetralogy boxset release. Guess I'll see.

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Mon Dec 21, 2009 1:00 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Versions on my PC and 1.01 DVD aside, I think I might just wait for a Gainax/Kara tetralogy boxset release. Guess I'll see.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Dec 21, 2009 1:21 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm going to feel weird owning 2-3 copies of this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Dec 21, 2009 5:44 am Not like I didn't post this a week ago but it's going to be great to FINALLY put the first film behind us for a while. For the first time since late 2006 we won't be waiting for anything Evangelion 1.0 related. Until they announce the Evangelion 1.5 release after the whole series has finished of course....

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Mon Dec 21, 2009 5:50 pm Possibly.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Dec 21, 2009 5:59 pm great nice to hear that there's a definitive release date for 1.11 in the states now...let's hope there's some bonus nuggets this time around

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Dec 21, 2009 6:40 pm Awesome, and I hope by then we will see 2.0 DVD news.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Dec 21, 2009 7:28 pm Eh, I'll wait until version 1.1111111111111111111111111111 comes out.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 21, 2009 8:02 pm Fazmotron wrote: Hopefully I will only have to wait a month or so to see an Australian release then.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Dec 21, 2009 10:24 pm Madman has got it all now, they recently released the dubbed 1.01, and have said on their forums that they'll be releasing 1.11 next year. RedAnt getting the rights was just a freak occurrence that was probably caused by the slow pick up of the 1.0 rights in America, and therefore is very unlikely to happen again.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 21, 2009 10:34 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm going to feel weird owning 2-3 copies of this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Dec 21, 2009 11:37 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm going to feel weird owning 2-3 copies of this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Dec 22, 2009 1:31 am I’ve got two different box sets of the TV series, standard and platinum, and two box sets of the original movies. This time around I only intend to by each of the new movies once, though I’m not entirely sure I can hold myself to that promise.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Dec 22, 2009 6:08 am Yeah... I try not to think about how much money I've sunk into 1.0 already. Let's just say the Rebuild franchise has not been a cheap affair, comparatively.
